steps.title: Distribute the monomial
steps.content:
Multiply the monomial by each term in the binomial, keeping all factors from the monomial with each product:
$$(x^{-5}y^4z^3)(x^2z^6+y^7z^{-5})=(x^{-5}y^4z^3)(x^2z^6)+(x^{-5}y^4z^3)(y^7z^{-5})$$

steps.title: Combine like bases
steps.content:
When powers have the same base, add their exponents. The first product becomes $x^{-5+2}y^4z^{3+6}$, and the second becomes $x^{-5}y^{4+7}z^{3+(-5)}$. So the product is
$$x^{-3}y^4z^9+x^{-5}y^{11}z^{-2}$$

steps.title: Match the product
steps.content:
The simplified expression keeps $y^4$ in the first term and keeps $x^{-5}$ with $y^{11}$ in the second term. The expression is
$$x^{-3}y^4z^9+x^{-5}y^{11}z^{-2}$$
The answer is D.
